paper_title: Multibeam Swath Bathymetry and Seismic-Reflection Surveys

methods.description: Process for acquiring multibeam swath bathymetry data off the west coast of Haida Gwaii and Dixon Entrance using advanced sonar systems to map the seafloor.

steps.description: Multibeam swath bathymetry was acquired using a hull-mounted, Kongsberg EM710 system.
steps.searchable_summary: acquire multibeam swath bathymetry data using Kongsberg EM710 system

steps.description: The data were edited for spurious bathymetric and navigational points and processed using CARIS software.
steps.searchable_summary: process and edit bathymetry data using CARIS software

steps.description: Multibeam data were gridded at 5 m resolution, exported as ASCII files, and imported into ArcInfo software for analysis and image production.
steps.searchable_summary: grid and visualize bathymetry data using ArcInfo

methods.description: Method for conducting seismic-reflection surveys and collecting sediment samples to study subsurface stratigraphy and seafloor composition.

steps.description: A Knudson low-power, 3.5 kHz CHIRP seismic-reflection profiling system was used to image subsurface stratigraphy.
A Benthos piston corer was used to collect seafloor sediment cores.
steps.searchable_summary: collect seafloor sediment cores using Benthos piston corer

steps.description: Cores were run through a Geotek Multi-Scan Core Logger to measure density, velocity, and magnetic susceptibility.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze sediment cores using Geotek Multi-Scan Core Logger

steps.description: Interpretation of seismic-reflection data was undertaken using Knudson Post Survey and Kingdom Suite software.
steps.searchable_summary: interpret seismic-reflection data using Kingdom Suite
